skill.name: amazon-product-search-api-skill
skill.description: Collects structured Amazon search result data through BrowserAct's hosted workflow API. It can search by keyword, filter by brand, choose a result limit, and request localized Amazon sessions.
skill.summary: Search Amazon listings and return structured product data through BrowserAct.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified network access, environment variable access, and agent-executed script behavior. I did not find evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection, but the skill sends a BrowserAct API key and user search parameters to an external service, so publication should include a clear credential and data-sharing warning.

medium_findings.title: External API Receives Credential and Search Parameters
medium_findings.description: The script builds an Authorization header from the BrowserAct API key and sends it with workflow requests to api.browseract.com. This appears aligned with the skill purpose, but it exposes credentials and user search terms to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network destination, Authorization header, and BrowserAct workflow calls are directly visible in the script. The use is legitimate for this skill, but the data-sharing risk is real.

medium_findings.title: Agent Is Instructed to Execute a Local Script
medium_findings.description: The skill documentation instructs the agent to run a Python command with user-provided keywords, brand, quantity, and language values. This is expected for the skill, but command construction should quote arguments and avoid shell interpolation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution instruction is explicit, and the script reads command line arguments. I did not find a subprocess call inside the script, so the risk depends on how the agent invokes it.

low_findings.title: API Key Access Is Documented Rather Than Hidden
low_findings.description: The skill declares and checks BROWSERACT_API_KEY and asks the user to configure it when missing. This is a sensitive environment variable, but there is no evidence that the key is read for unrelated purposes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable name and missing-key handling are visible in the files. The remaining concern is limited to normal credential handling for the BrowserAct service.

low_findings.title: Markdown Formatting Caused Many External Command False Positives
low_findings.description: Most SKILL.md detections are inline backticks around parameter names, examples, and output fields. These are documentation formatting, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown descriptions and lists. They do not execute code by themselves.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged SKILL.md lines describe an API template, example product brands, and pricing fields. I did not find cryptographic code, hashing, or weak algorithm use in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are prose and parameter documentation. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic implementation was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Sent to Third-Party API
dangerous_patterns.description: The script constructs a Bearer token header from the BrowserAct API key and uses it in HTTP requests. This is necessary for the workflow but must be disclosed to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The header construction and outbound request are direct code evidence. The destination is a known BrowserAct API endpoint used by the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automated Polling of External Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The script polls BrowserAct task status and sends the same Authorization header during polling. This increases exposure duration for the credential during execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The polling loop and authenticated GET request are visible in the script. The behavior is expected, but it is still an external network pattern.

content.value_statement: Amazon product research can be slow when users need structured listing data across many products. This skill runs a BrowserAct workflow to collect titles, prices, ratings, availability, and related listing fields.
actual_capabilities: Runs a local Python script that calls BrowserAct's workflow API.
Searches Amazon listings by keyword and optional brand filter.
Sets a maximum product count for paginated search collection.
Requests localized sessions with supported language codes.
Returns product fields such as title, URL, price, rating, reviews, delivery, and availability.
Prints task status messages while the BrowserAct workflow is running.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY before execution.
Sends search terms and the BrowserAct API key to BrowserAct.
Depends on BrowserAct availability and the configured workflow template.
The current script returns after the polling timeout path, so result retrieval may need review.

use_cases.title: Compare Category Prices
use_cases.description: Collect current prices and list prices for a product category to identify common discount ranges.

use_cases.title: Track Competitor Listings
use_cases.description: Search for a brand and review listing titles, ratings, review counts, and availability.

use_cases.title: Build Product Research Lists
use_cases.description: Gather product URLs and basic listing details for a catalog research spreadsheet.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Amazon for wireless earbuds and return up to 20 results in English.

prompt_templates.title: Brand Filtered Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Filter a query by a specific brand.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Sony wireless earbuds on Amazon. Limit the search to 30 products and include prices, ratings, and availability.

prompt_templates.title: Localized Market Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a supported language code for localized results.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Amazon for laptop stands from Amazon Basics using Japanese language settings. Return up to 25 products.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Research Dataset
prompt_templates.scenario: Collect a larger structured dataset for analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Collect up to 100 Samsung phone listings from Amazon. Summarize price ranges, review counts, Best Seller markers, and availability.

output_examples.input: Search Amazon for Sony wireless earbuds, maximum 20 results.
output_examples.output: A structured product list with item titles, product URLs, current prices, ratings, review counts, delivery details, and availability status.

output_examples.input: Find Samsung phones and highlight Best Seller listings.
output: Summary of matching Samsung phone listings.
Price, rating, review count, and availability details included for comparison.

output_examples.input: Search Amazon in Japanese for laptop stands.
output_examples.output: Localized listing results using the requested language setting, including titles, prices, shipping information, and product URLs.

best_practices: Use precise keywords and a brand filter to reduce irrelevant listings.
Start with a small result limit before collecting larger datasets.
Review BrowserAct API usage and data policies before sending sensitive searches.
anti_patterns: Do not paste a BrowserAct API key into chat when an environment variable is available.
Do not request very large collections when a sample can answer the question.
Do not treat estimated monthly sales as verified sales data without validation.

faq.question: Does this skill need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY to call the BrowserAct workflow API.

faq.question: Does it scrape Amazon directly from my machine?
faq.answer: No. The local script sends a workflow request to BrowserAct, which handles the browser workflow.

faq.question: What product fields can it return?
faq.answer: It can return title, URL, rating, review count, price, delivery details, shipping location, Best Seller status, and availability.

faq.question: Can it search in different languages?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented language options include English, German, French, Italian, Spanish, Japanese, and Chinese variants.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with warnings. Users should know it sends credentials and search parameters to BrowserAct.

faq.question: What should I check before using it?
faq.answer: Confirm the BrowserAct API key, choose a reasonable result limit, and review whether external processing is acceptable.
